title: Grondorn Muse

{{Quote|That Grondorn—talented, I grant you, but he couldn't remember to put his shirt on unless he got a written order.|Pytor Tuko|<ref name="SWG" />}}
'''Grondorn Muse''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] member of the [[Corellian militia]], who eventually left and became a popular [[Folk music|folk]] [[Musician/Legends|singer]] before the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] blacklisted him for the [[Politics/Legends|political]] content of his [[Music/Legends|songs]]. By [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Muse had joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Muse attended a briefing held in the Alliance's [[Great Temple/Legends|headquarters]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. Some [[Time/Legends|time]] following the battle, Muse rejoined the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] militia, and he played in the [[Fighting 357th Serenaders]] [[Band/Legends|band]]. While returning to his base in the [[City/Legends|town]] of [[Bela Vistal/Legends|Bela Vistal]] following a training exercise, Muse realized he had left his [[Kloo horn/Legends|kloo horn]] behind. He hired a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]], who helped him retrieve his instrument, and brought it back to Bela Vistal. Muse then realized he had forgotten to deliver a [[Datacard/Legends|datadisc]] of the anthem "[[Corellia Ho!]]" to [[Pytor Tuko]], the leader of another band, the [[Battling 166th Troubadours]]. He then paid the spacer to make the delivery for him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Look, you try herding a pack of wayward Serenaders out in the middle of nowhere, and you'll lose track of details too. I hope if you can get the band member and my horn back safely, I won't get in trouble with my commander. Will you bring him here?|Grondorn Muse|<ref name="SWG" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="anh" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]],<ref name="SWG" /> Grondorn Muse was a member of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia's]] [[Corellian militia|militia]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. At some point, he [[Marriage/Legends|married]] a woman by the name of [[Duriet]].<ref name="ccg" /> He eventually left the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] militia, and established himself as a [[Folk music|folk]] [[Musician/Legends|singer]], famous for his [[Music/Legends|songs]] "[[Ballad of Stars' End]]" and "[[Those Ole Spacer's Blues]]," and for breaking out in the traditional [[Space Pirate Boogie]] to close his performances.<ref name="Slugthrowers">{{SW|url=news/slugthrowers-part-2|series=Slugthrowers: An Overview of Popular Music and Musicians in a Galaxy Far, Far Away|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> A popular musician, the [[Politics/Legends|political]] content of his songs resulted in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] blacklisting him.<ref name="ccg" /> By the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].</ref> Muse joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] along with Duriet.<ref name="ccg" />

[[File:TempleofYavin4-AoRCR.png|thumb|right|300px|The Great Temple, where Grondorn Muse was present before the Battle of Yavin]]
In 0 BBY,<ref name="NEC" /> Muse was present in the [[Great Temple/Legends|Great Temple]], the Alliance's main headquarters on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Muse attended a briefing held by the base's [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], who discussed the Alliance's plans to attack the Imperial [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|DS-1 Orbital Battle Station]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] after a weakness had been discovered in the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station's]] [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]]. Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were sent to destroy the [[Space station/Legends|station]] by firing [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedos]] into its exhaust port. The battle was a major victory for the Alliance when [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] managed to successfully destroy the battlestation.<ref name="anh" />

Sometime following the Battle of Yavin, Muse returned to Corellia. By that point, he was once again a member of the militia in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Bela Vistal/Legends|Bela Vistal]], and he played the [[Kloo horn/Legends|kloo horn]] in a militia [[Band/Legends|band]] known as the [[Fighting 357th Serenaders]]. Around this [[Time/Legends|time]], Muse and his fellow band members went on a training exercise out in the wilds. After they returned to base, Muse discovered that he had forgotten his kloo horn. [[Vincol Dunker]], one of his bandmates, went to retrieve the kloo horn, but he got lost in the wilderness.<ref name="SWG" />

{{Gamemechanics}}
[[File:BelaVistal.png|thumb|left|300px|Muse joined the Fighting 357th Serenaders band in the city of Bela Vistal (pictured).]]
Fortunately for Muse, he encountered a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] who was willing to help. Muse asked the spacer to find the band member and his kloo horn, so that he would not get in trouble with his [[Unidentified Corellian militia commander|commander]]. The spacer agreed, and successfully set out to find Dunker, bringing him and the kloo horn safely back to Muse in Bela Vistal in return for 750 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="SWG" />

However, Muse required more assistance from the spacer. When Dunker was brought back to Bela Vistal, he gave Muse a [[Datacard/Legends|datadisc]] with a new arrangement of the popular anthem "[[Corellia Ho!]]," which Muse had promised to deliver to [[Pytor Tuko]], the band leader of the [[Battling 166th Troubadours]]. Muse had completely forgotten about the arrangement, and he could not deliver the datadisc himself, as he had to wait in town for new militia orders. Muse asked the spacer to deliver it for him, offering payment in return. After hearing Muse recite a portion of the tune, the spacer agreed to carry out the task. After finding Tuko and giving him the datadisc, the spacer returned to Muse and was awarded with 1,000 credits. Muse mentioned that the Battling 166th Troubadours could now hold their rehearsals in the wilderness, and after the task's completion, he informed the spacer that the militia situation was now under control.<ref name="SWG" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Thanks. The band member's name is Vincol Dunker. He found my kloo horn, so don't let anything happen to him. That might hurt the horn. Sorry, just joking.|Grondorn Muse|<ref name="SWG" />}}
As a musician, Grondorn Muse was blacklisted by the Empire for the political content of his songs.<ref name="ccg" /> He was described by his comrades in the Corellian militia as "forgetful;" he managed to both leave behind his kloo horn on a training exercise, and forget to deliver the "Corellia Ho!" disc to Pytor Tuko. When the spacer pressed Muse by asking him why he forgot his kloo horn, he answered defensively that herding a pack of wayward Serenaders out in the middle of nowhere was no easy task. He also had a penchant for making jokes, such as when he implied to the spacer that the safety of his kloo horn was more important than that of his fellow band member, Vincol Dunker. Likewise, he commented on the quality of rehearsals performed by the Battling 166th Troubadours by saying it was better for them to play in the wilds.<ref name="SWG" /> Muse also possessed a certain level of [[Force-attunement]].<ref name="ccg" /> At the time of the Battle of Yavin, he had [[Color/Legends|blond]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], blue [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and fair skin.<ref name="anh" /> By his time with the Fighting 357th Serenaders, Muse had become bald with gray eyebrows, and his skin had a darker tone.<ref name="SWG" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:GrondornMuse.png|thumb|right|Grondorn Muse, as he appeared in ''Star Wars Galaxies'']]
Portrayed by an uncredited [[Dave Holland]],<ref name="ConGuests">{{WebCite|url=http://www.convention-guests.com/html/more_star_wars.html|text=More Star Wars|work=Convention-guests.com|archivedate=20100411050157}}</ref> Grondorn Muse first appeared in [[1977]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] as a background character during the briefing prior to the Rebel assault on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]].<ref name="anh" /> He was given a name, and his backstory was slightly expanded, in the ''[[Special Edition Limited]]'' set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].<ref name="ccg" /> The name "Grondorn Muse" is an anagram of Gordon Sumner, the singer better known as [[Wikipedia:Sting (musician)|Sting]].<ref name="Sting article">{{WebCite|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/women/womens-business/10754984/Trudie-Styler-interview-I-get-a-lot-of-mud-slung-at-me.html|text=Trudie Styler interview: 'I get a lot of mud slung at me'|date=2014-04-13, 7:00AM BST|work=The Telegraph|archivedate=20170814025030}}</ref>

Muse later appeared as a [[Wikipedia:Non-player character|non-player character]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Players who performed a series of [[:w:c:swg:Quest|quests]] for Muse had to escort Vincol Dunker to Bela Vistal, and deliver a datadisc to Pytor Tuko outside of town. In the game, Muse's appearance differed significantly from that of his depiction in the ''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game. In ''Galaxies'', Muse had gray eyes. Although the player could choose not to do Muse's quests, this article assumes 100% game completion.<ref name="SWG" />
